name: DriveTime
description_short: Used car dealer specializing in subprime auto loans. 145+ dealerships. In-house financing for all credit levels.
description_long:
DriveTime is one of the largest used car dealership networks in the US, with over 145 locations across 26 states. Founded in 2002 (originally as Ugly Duckling in 1992) and headquartered in Tempe, Arizona, DriveTime specializes in providing auto financing to consumers with challenged credit — including bankruptcies, repossessions, and no credit history.

Unlike traditional dealerships that rely on third-party lenders, DriveTime uses in-house financing through its affiliated company Bridgecrest, giving them flexibility to approve buyers that banks would decline. There's no minimum credit score requirement, though rates are higher for higher-risk borrowers.

Every DriveTime vehicle undergoes a multi-point inspection and comes with a limited warranty. The company also partners with SilverRock for extended warranty and vehicle service contract options.

DriveTime's online tools include a pre-qualification form (soft pull only), payment calculator, and inventory search filtered by price, make, model, and body type. You can start the process online and complete it in-store.
pros: No minimum credit score — approves challenged credit
Pre-qualification is a soft pull only
cons: Higher interest rates than traditional auto loans
Vehicle selection limited to their inventory
May require larger down payment for lower credit scores
Some negative customer reviews about vehicle quality
best_for: Buying a car after bankruptcy or repossession
People who prefer in-person dealership experience with guaranteed financing
services: Subprime auto financing (no minimum credit score)
